substep.title: Flip Plan Status to Locked
substep.action: Edit each plan doc frontmatter: status: "active" → status: "accepted". Note: the plan-as-living-doc per Lock 10 has the I-phase implementation log embedded — those subsections are appended later (during I-phase) without un-locking the structural plan content.

substep.title: Link to Structure
substep.action: Edit {{structure_path}} frontmatter children: add each plan's canonical alias.

substep.title: Stamp
substep.action: For each plan file, stamp similarly.

substep.title: Recommend Compaction (Lock 20)
substep.output:
P-phase locked. Bundle complete for the planning session.
Per Lock 20, the workflow is designed as a TWO-SESSION split: - Planning session (this one): orient → grill → design → structure → plan - Execution session (next): re-orient on the bundle directory → implement → verify
Recommend compacting THIS session now. A fresh execution session will load the bundle from disk and resume cleanly.
- Compact now (Recommended): run /handoff to write a portable handoff, then trigger compact - Continue in this session: proceed directly to worktree-setup + code-implement (lower budget; may need to handoff mid-flow)

substep.title: Emit Handoff
substep.template-output:
P-phase complete.
- Plans: {{plan_count}} ({{plan_paths}}) - Slices: {{slice_count}} - Parallel-safety: {{decision}}
**Next** (in this session or after compact): 1. Invoke `worktree-setup` for the first plan to execute (single-plan: invoke once; multi-plan parallel: invoke N times; multi-plan sequential: invoke once per plan in dependency order). 2. Invoke `code-implement` with the worktree handoff.
OR (recommended per Lock 20): compact session; the next codesmith invocation in execution mode (bundle directory as input) will resume from this state.
